disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Acute hot/swollen single joint, unable to weight-bear — cannot be examined or aspirated remotely to exclude septic arthritis
trigger_question_examples: Is it one joint, hot and swollen, that you can't put weight on?
How quickly did it come up?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, it's one area (left ankle/midfoot), swollen and sore, and I can't weight-bear.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No high fever/rigors/systemic toxicity currently; big toe (1st MTP) spared; known seropositive palindromic pattern
trigger_question_examples: Any fever, shakes, or feeling generally unwell?
Is your big toe involved, and is this like your usual fleeting flares?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever or shakes, my big toe isn't involved, and this is like my usual flares but worse.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: It's just my palindromic flare, right? Can I just take anti-inflammatories at home?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Explain that although a palindromic flare is likely, an acute hot swollen single joint that you cannot put weight on must be examined in person today to exclude a septic joint (which can only be excluded by aspiration), so same-day assessment is needed alongside interim NSAID/RICE, with a strong septic-arthritis safety-net — rather than home-only reassurance.
